description_long: This object depicts a small graveyard scene featuring three prominent wooden crosses arranged in a row, each with a sculpted figure affixed to them, reminiscent of a crucifixion tableau. At the base of the central cross, two kneeling figures are present, appearing to be in mourning or prayer, while a third figure stands nearby. The ground is covered with textured, grassy terrain, giving the impression of a natural, slightly uneven outdoor setting. The crosses are weathered and roughly hewn, with visible bark and irregularities, suggesting they are made from unprocessed timber. The figures and crosses are rendered in a stone-like or concrete material, contrasting with the earthy base. No visible inscriptions or text are present on the crosses or the ground.
description: A graveyard scene with three wooden crosses, sculpted figures in mourning, and a grassy, natural terrain base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three crosses with figures affixed and two kneeling figures at the base of the central cross.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the crosses and the kneeling figures, with less detail visible on the figures themselves.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the crosses and figures, with the arrangement of the scene more apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the linear arrangement of the crosses and the figures' positions.
